name: powerlifted-htg-grounder-work
gate: relaxed: every baseline-solved pair still solves with plan cost <= baseline; trajectory not gated
note: deterministic grounder_atoms is a SCREEN, not a gate: 'cheaper-per-fact' wins leave it flat while cutting time

description: baseline: master HEAD + grounder meter (identical code to greedy-join baseline); current Fast-Downward grounder; 5 sweeps via REPS=5 ./autoresearch.sh (gate passed all 5). grounder_atoms identical on every sweep (deterministic).

Defer achiever construction: probe reached_facts with the achiever-free head, build the Achievers vector ONLY on the new-fact branch (cheaper-path keeps and discards never read an achiever -- backchaining reads achievers from lp, written once at first insertion; reached_facts achievers are dead). Moved the cheapest-path check inline into project/join/product (removed the batch newfacts buffer), move-insert kept facts into reached_facts. grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT vs baseline (identical productions => behavior-preserving; 62/62 checks pass). Win is 'cheaper' not 'fewer'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD (52.976 median): KEEP, confidence 9.09x, 5.87%. peak_mem 68->64MB.

Vendor utils/small_vector.h (inline-buffer vector for trivially-copyable T) and back datalog::Arguments with small_vector<Term,4> instead of std::vector<Term>. Typical atom arities (genome-edit-distance, the dominant ~59% domain, has arity <=2) now keep their argument terms inline, removing the per-produced-fact heap allocation (~47.6M Arguments copies in join/project/product). small_vector operator== is element-wise via Term::operator== and iterators are raw Term*, so Fact hashing/equality is byte-for-byte identical -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT (deterministic counters unchanged; behavior preserved; 62/62 checks pass). Fixed join.h JoiningVariables to qualify std::find/std::distance (raw-pointer iterators kill the prior std ADL). Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD cf27d27 (49.697 median): KEEP, confidence 126x, 20.2%. Note: an earlier 65s single-sweep reading was contaminated by leftover background perf+search processes (env blocks perf sampling: 0 samples); killed them, load settled, re-measured clean.

Back datalog::Achievers with utils::small_vector<int,2> instead of std::vector<int>, and add direct int(a) and int(a,b) constructors so the hot project (1-atom body) and join (2-atom body) achiever bodies are built inline with NO heap allocation -- previously each newly reached fact (~22.4M) allocated a 1-2 element int vector. Product rules (body >2) keep a std::vector-range constructor and spill transparently. Achievers are not part of Fact hash/equality, so grounder_atoms/pushes stay FLAT (deterministic; behavior preserved; 62/62 checks pass; plan costs unchanged). Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD fe9b548 (39.588 median): KEEP, confidence 81.5x, 16.3%. peak_mem 64->55MB.

Change JoinHashKey from std::vector<int> to utils::small_vector<int,3>; the key is rebuilt once per (popped fact, matching join rule) and joins share few vars (genome-edit-distance joins on <=2), so it now stays inline (no heap) both as the per-firing probe key and as the stored map key. Added a local JoinHashKeyHash (utils::hash_range, identical hash values to the prior VectorHash<int>) since utils::VectorHash is hard-typed to std::vector; added small_vector::reserve. hash/eq identical -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT (behavior preserved; 62/62 checks pass). Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD 924e16e (32.878 median): KEEP, confidence 31.5x, 10.1%.
